prior.content_md:
**MONITORING — Rescission rulemaking reference (legal status uncertain).**
The EPA source page as of February 13, 2026 lists a related item: "Final Rule: Rescission of the Greenhouse Gas Endangerment Finding and Motor Vehicle Greenhouse Gas Emission Standards Under the Clean Air Act." This reference appears on the EPA regulatory page alongside the Phase 3 materials.

*Research gap: The current legal status of the Phase 3 rule — specifically whether any rescission action has been finalized, stayed, or litigated — cannot be confirmed from the available sources in this input context. This is a material gap for the workspace's client communications and internal operations planning. Legal Confirmation Required before the workspace represents Phase 3 requirements as currently enforceable in any client-facing context.*

The Sabin Center for Climate Change Law at Columbia University (https://climate.law.columbia.edu) tracks climate litigation and regulatory rollback actions and is a candidate source for monitoring Phase 3 legal status. *This source is identified as a candidate; no specific Phase 3 litigation or rescission finding has been confirmed from available sources in this context.*

*Source: EPA, "Regulations for Greenhouse Gas Emissions from Commercial Trucks & Buses," last updated February 13, 2026. https://www.epa.gov/regulations-emissions-vehicles-and-engines/regulations-greenhouse-gas-emissions-commercial-trucks*
